steps.title: Recognize the temporal order from the dates
steps.content: The UK march (October 12 – November 16, 1912) and the US march (December 16 – December 28, 1912) establish a clear sequence: UK first, US second. The student must emphasize this order.

steps.title: Apply the order to find the answer
steps.content:
Choice A begins with “After” and structures the sentence to show the UK march first, then the US march—directly expressing the chronological order.

Choices B, C, and D either treat both marches as simultaneous (B), reverse the sequence (C), or mention only one march (D).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
